name: retail_sales_partial_join
description: Retail sales regression problem built from a fact table plus three dimension tables that must be joined before training. The target is revenue_eur. Four source files must be uploaded together: retail_sales.csv (fact, 320 rows), product_catalog.csv (join: retail_sales.product_id = product_catalog.sku), store_directory.csv (join: retail_sales.store_id = store_directory.store_code), and customer_segments.csv (join: retail_sales.segment_code = customer_segments.segment_code). The join keys have DIFFERENT column names across files, so they must be matched semantically. This scenario stress-tests partial-coverage joins: the product and customer dimensions contain more keys than the fact table uses (foreign key is a subset of the primary key), and the store directory is MISSING several stores referenced by the fact table (orphan foreign keys), so a left join will leave some store attributes null. Used to validate how join discovery reports coverage, containment and cardinality, and how the controller tolerates incomplete referential integrity.

columns.name: txn_id
columns.description: Unique transaction identifier. Not a join key. Source: retail_sales.csv.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: Product foreign key (e.g. SKU001). Join key to product_catalog.sku. Source: retail_sales.csv.

columns.name: store_id
columns.description: Store foreign key (e.g. ST01). Join key to store_directory.store_code. Some values have no matching store (orphan FK). Source: retail_sales.csv.

columns.name: segment_code
columns.description: Customer segment foreign key (A-D). Join key to customer_segments.segment_code. Source: retail_sales.csv.

columns.name: units
columns.description: Units sold in the transaction. Source: retail_sales.csv.

columns.name: promo_flag
columns.description: 1 if the transaction had a promotion, else 0. Source: retail_sales.csv.

columns.name: revenue_eur
columns.description: Transaction revenue in euros. TARGET COLUMN. Source: retail_sales.csv.

columns.name: category
columns.description: Product category. Source: product_catalog.csv (joined on product_id = sku). Full coverage.

columns.name: unit_price_eur
columns.description: Catalogue unit price in euros. Source: product_catalog.csv (joined on product_id = sku).

columns.name: brand
columns.description: Product brand. Source: product_catalog.csv (joined on product_id = sku).

columns.name: region
columns.description: Store region. Source: store_directory.csv (joined on store_id = store_code). Orphan stores absent from the directory must be imputed (mode) before training.

columns.name: store_size_sqm
columns.description: Store sales-floor area in square metres. Source: store_directory.csv (joined on store_id = store_code). Orphan stores must be imputed (mean) before training.

columns.name: store_type
columns.description: Store format. Source: store_directory.csv (joined on store_id = store_code). Orphan stores must be imputed (mode) before training.

columns.name: segment_name
columns.description: Customer segment name. Source: customer_segments.csv (joined on segment_code). Full coverage.

columns.name: avg_income_eur
columns.description: Average income for the customer segment. Source: customer_segments.csv (joined on segment_code).
